Example-English 1: The friendly boy shared his cookies with the new student.
Example-Chinnese 1: 那個友善的男孩和新學生分享他的餅乾。
Example-English 2: Everyone likes him because he is a very friendly boy who always smiles.
Example-Chinnese 2: 大家都喜歡他，因為他是一個總是微笑的非常友善的男孩。
Example-English 3: The friendly boy helped the old lady today.
Example-Chinnese 3: 那位友善的男孩今天幫了老太太。

Example-English 1: The teacher stands in front of the blackboard to teach the class.
Example-Chinnese 1: 老師站在黑板前面教課。
Example-English 2: Please park your car in front of the supermarket.
Example-Chinnese 2: 請把你的車停在超市前面。
Example-English 3: There is a garden in front of my house.
Example-Chinnese 3: 我家前面有一個美麗的花園。

Example-English 1: I cannot decide to wear the red shirt or the blue shirt.
Example-Chinnese 1: 我無法決定要穿紅襯衫還是藍襯衫。
Example-English 2: They decide to go to the zoo to see the monkeys.
Example-Chinnese 2: 他們決定去動物園看猴子。
Example-English 3: We must decide to start the art project today.
Example-Chinnese 3: 我們必須決定今天開始美術專案。

Example-English 1: He is very interested in outer space and wants to be an astronaut.
Example-Chinnese 1: 他對外太空非常感興趣，並想成為一名太空人。
Example-English 2: My sister is interested in learning how to play the guitar.
Example-Chinnese 2: 我妹妹對學習彈吉他感興趣。
Example-English 3: I am interested in the school basketball team.
Example-Chinnese 3: 我對學校籃球隊有興趣。

Example-English 1: The grandfather told an interesting story about a brave knight and a dragon.
Example-Chinnese 1: 祖父講了一個關於勇敢騎士和龍的有趣故事。
Example-English 2: I read an interesting story in the comic book yesterday.
Example-Chinnese 2: 我昨天在漫畫書裡讀到一個有趣的故事。
Example-English 3: My brother told an interesting story last night.
Example-Chinnese 3: 我哥哥昨晚講了一個有趣的故事。

Definition: to travel around a new place
Example-English 1: The children put on their swimsuits to explore the pool in the backyard.
Example-Chinnese 1: 孩子們穿上泳衣，去探索後院的水池。
Example-English 2: We can use a flashlight to explore the pool at night.
Example-Chinnese 2: 我們可以用手電筒在晚上探索這個水池。
Example-English 3: The frog jumped in to explore the pool.
Example-Chinnese 3: 青蛙跳進水裡探索這個水池。

Definition: a tool used for eating food
Example-English 1: It is polite to eat your spaghetti with a fork, not with your hands.
Example-Chinnese 1: 用叉子吃義大利麵是有禮貌的，不要用手。
Example-English 2: She picked up the piece of cake with a fork.
Example-Chinnese 2: 她用叉子叉起那塊蛋糕。
Example-English 3: Please eat your salad with a fork today.
Example-Chinnese 3: 今天請用叉子吃沙拉。

Example-English 1: The treasure chest is full of shiny gold coins and diamonds.
Example-Chinnese 1: 寶藏箱裡裝滿了閃亮的金幣和鑽石。
Example-English 2: The classroom is full of noisy students waiting for the bell to ring.
Example-Chinnese 2: 教室裡擠滿了吵鬧的學生，等著鐘聲響起。
Example-English 3: After rain the bucket is full of water.
Example-Chinnese 3: 大雨後，水桶裡裝滿了水。

Definition: something you wear to cover your hand
Example-English 1: I took off my wet glove after playing in the snow for an hour.
Example-Chinnese 1: 在雪地裡玩了一小時後，我脫下了濕手套。
Example-English 2: Do not touch the clean clothes with your wet glove.
Example-Chinnese 2: 不要用你的濕手套碰乾淨的衣服。
Example-English 3: He put his wet glove near the heater.
Example-Chinnese 3: 他把濕手套放在暖氣機旁邊。

Example-English 1: You must whisper to your friend in the library so you do not make loud noises.
Example-Chinnese 1: 你必須在圖書館裡對朋友低語，這樣才不會發出很大的噪音。
Example-English 2: The spy had to whisper to his partner to keep the secret safe.
Example-Chinnese 2: 間諜必須對他的夥伴低語，以確保秘密安全。
Example-English 3: You must whisper to your friend in class.
Example-Chinnese 3: 上課時你必須對朋友低語。

Example-English 1: The fisherman was surprised when he caught an enormous fish in the small river.
Example-Chinnese 1: 漁夫在小河裡抓到一條巨大的魚時感到很驚訝。
Example-English 2: We saw an enormous fish swimming in the deep ocean at the aquarium.
Example-Chinnese 2: 我們在水族館看到一條巨大的魚在深海裡游泳。
Example-English 3: They caught an enormous fish in the river.
Example-Chinnese 3: 他們在河裡抓到一條巨大的魚。

Example-English 1: Going to the beach on such a hot day is an excellent idea!
Example-Chinnese 1: 在這麼熱的天氣去海灘是一個極好的主意！
Example-English 2: The manager said my plan for the new project was an excellent idea.
Example-Chinnese 2: 經理說我對新專案的計畫是一個極好的主意。
Example-English 3: That is an excellent idea for our school trip.
Example-Chinnese 3: 那是我們學校旅行的一個極好主意。

Definition: to go and bring something back
Example-English 1: The smart dog ran across the yard to fetch the ring for its owner.
Example-Chinnese 1: 聰明的狗跑過院子，為主人撿回那枚戒指。
Example-English 2: Please go upstairs and fetch the ring from my jewelry box.
Example-Chinnese 2: 請上樓去從我的珠寶盒裡拿那枚戒指。
Example-English 3: The boy ran to fetch the ring quickly.
Example-Chinnese 3: 男孩快跑過去撿回那枚戒指。

Example-English 1: The child cried when he saw a frightening crab with huge claws on the beach.
Example-Chinnese 1: 小孩在海灘上看到一隻有著巨大鉗子的可怕螃蟹時哭了。
Example-English 2: I did not want to touch the frightening crab hiding under the rock.
Example-Chinnese 2: 我不想碰那隻躲在石頭下可怕的螃蟹。
Example-English 3: That frightening crab moved towards us fast.
Example-Chinnese 3: 那隻可怕的螃蟹快速朝我們移動。

Example-English 1: The king gave the queen a very expensive ring with a large diamond.
Example-Chinnese 1: 國王給了王后一枚鑲有大鑽石的非常昂貴的戒指。
Example-English 2: He saved money for two years to buy that expensive ring.
Example-Chinnese 2: 他存了兩年的錢來買那枚昂貴的戒指。
Example-English 3: Keep your expensive ring in this small box.
Example-Chinnese 3: 把這枚昂貴的戒指放在這個小盒子裡。

Example-English 1: The math teacher will explain to the students how to solve this difficult problem.
Example-Chinnese 1: 數學老師將向學生們解釋如何解決這個難題。
Example-English 2: Can you explain to me why you are late for school today?
Example-Chinnese 2: 你能向我解釋為什麼你今天上學遲到嗎？
Example-English 3: I need to explain to my mom after school.
Example-Chinnese 3: 放學後我需要向媽媽解釋。

Definition: to give an answer when you don't know
Example-English 1: Guess what I have inside this mysterious black box!
Example-Chinnese 1: 猜猜看我這個神秘的黑盒子裡有什麼！
Example-English 2: You will never guess what happened to me on my way home.
Example-Chinnese 2: 你絕對猜不到我回家的路上發生了什麼事。
Example-English 3: Tom can guess what is in the black box.
Example-Chinnese 3: 湯姆能猜出黑盒子裡有什麼。

Definition: the man that a woman is married to
Example-English 1: The woman and her husband went to the romantic restaurant for their anniversary.
Example-Chinnese 1: 那個女人和她的丈夫去浪漫的餐廳慶祝週年紀念日。
Example-English 2: Her husband bought her a beautiful bouquet of red roses.
Example-Chinnese 2: 她的丈夫給她買了一束美麗的紅玫瑰。
Example-English 3: She asked her husband to carry the heavy bags.
Example-Chinnese 3: 她請丈夫幫忙提重袋子。

Definition: the woman that a man is married to
Example-English 1: The man cooked a special dinner to surprise his wife on her birthday.
Example-Chinnese 1: 那個男人做了一頓特別的晚餐，想在他妻子的生日給她一個驚喜。
Example-English 2: His wife works as a doctor in the city hospital.
Example-Chinnese 2: 他的妻子在市立醫院當醫生。
Example-English 3: He bought nice flowers for his wife today.
Example-Chinnese 3: 他今天為妻子買了漂亮的花。

Example-English 1: The pirate map shows the location of a very important treasure hidden on the island.
Example-Chinnese 1: 海盜地圖顯示了隱藏在島上一個非常重要的寶藏的位置。
Example-English 2: This old necklace is an important treasure to our family history.
Example-Chinnese 2: 這條舊項鍊對我們家族歷史來說是一個重要的寶藏。
Example-English 3: The museum keeps the important treasure inside.
Example-Chinnese 3: 博物館把這件重要寶物保存在裡面。

Example-English 1: My grandmother gave me this necklace, and I will keep the ring safely in my box forever.
Example-Chinnese 1: 我祖母給了我這條項鍊，我會永遠把這枚戒指安全地保留在我的盒子裡。
Example-English 2: The princess decided to keep the ring as a memory of the brave knight.
Example-Chinnese 2: 公主決定保留這枚戒指，作為對勇敢騎士的紀念。
Example-English 3: You can keep the ring in this wooden box.
Example-Chinnese 3: 你可以把戒指放在這個木盒子裡。
